building.towers[*].floors — brochure does not state floor counts numerically. Schema requires positive integer; placeholder '30' kept here. NEEDS-CLIENT-INPUT.

neighbourhood.title: The Neighbourhood
paragraphs: The Forest Reserve is part of the Krisumi City Group Housing Colony in Sector 36A, Gurugram, on land admeasuring 6.4868 acres across two RERA registrations: 5.1743 acres for The Forest Reserve (Phase I) and 1.3125 acres for The Forest Reserve - II. The site sits along the Dwarka Expressway (Northern Peripheral Road) corridor with the Central Peripheral Road within minutes of the project entrance.

From the brochure's location map, key destinations are: Indira Gandhi International Airport at 24.8 km, Cyber City at 17 km, IFFCO Chowk at 13.6 km, MG Road at 14.6 km, Medanta Hospital at 9.6 km, Fortis Hospital at 9.2 km, and Hero Honda Chowk at 5.6 km. The Northern Peripheral Road frontage is at 1 km and Pataudi Road at 1.6 km.
The Forest Reserve is the latest Krisumi project in Sector 36A. Other Krisumi projects shown in the brochure are Waterfall Residences, Waterfall Suites, Waterfall Suites II, and Waterside Residences.
highlights: Sector 36A, Gurugram — part of the Krisumi City masterplan

cards.label: Indo-Japanese real estate development
cards.body: Krisumi Corporation is the joint venture between Sumitomo Corporation and the Krishna Group, with architecture by Nikken Sekkei (brochure page 7).

cards.label: Krisumi City masterplan
cards.body: Group Housing Colony in Sector 36A, Gurugram, with The Forest Reserve and The Forest Reserve - II as the latest phases (brochure page 63).

cards.label: Chinju No Mori — sacred grove
cards.body: The Forest Reserve is built on the Japanese concept of Chinju No Mori — a sacred haven for kami (brochure page 20).

builder.name: Krisumi Corporation
builder.description: Krisumi Corporation Private Limited is the developer of the Krisumi City Group Housing Colony in Sector 36A, Gurugram. The Forest Reserve and The Forest Reserve - II are part of this colony, registered separately with HARERA Gurugram (brochure page 63). The architectural design is by Nikken Sekkei, Japan's No. 1 architectural firm (brochure page 7). The brochure positions Krisumi as a partnership between Krishna Group — one of India's largest automotive component manufacturers, with Krishna Maruti Limited as its flagship company and 17 Joint Ventures with global leaders (brochure page 4) — and Sumitomo Corporation, the 400-year-old Sumitomo group, a Fortune 500 company with annual revenues of $47 Billion USD (FY 2024-25), 80,000 employees across 900 group companies in 65 countries, and over 300 world-class projects delivered across Japan, USA, China and Indonesia (brochure page 5). Krisumi's approach is guided by five Japanese principles: Wa (Harmony), Omotenashi (Hospitality), Monozukuri (Craftsmanship), Seijitsu (Integrity), and Iki (Sophistication) — brochure page 3.

investmentThesis.headline: Brochure-grounded summary
paragraphs: The Forest Reserve introduces itself with a single Japanese concept on brochure page 20: Chinju No Mori — a grove or forest that serves as a sacred haven for kami, the divine spirits that safeguard the land and its people. Within this sanctuary, nature, humanity and spirituality dwell in perfect harmony.

The brochure expresses the design philosophy through thirteen Japanese principles arranged across pages 22, 24, 26, 28, and 30: Minamoto (The Source), Engawa (Veranda), Kaiyu Sei (Strollable Gardens), Shakkei (The Bigger Picture), Mie Gakure (Show and Hide), In-ei (Light and Dark), Ten Sen Men (Point · Line · Surface), Seijaku (Stillness), Miyama (Deep Forest), Kagami (Mirror), Kekkai (Boundary), Koshi (Latticework), and Oku (Embracing the Secrecy).

The development is registered with HARERA Gurugram under two registrations: The Forest Reserve (RC/REP/HARERA/GGM/944/676/2025/47, 5.1743 acres, 298 residential units plus 53 EWS units) and The Forest Reserve - II (RC/REP/HARERA/GGM/945/677/2025/48, 1.3125 acres, 244 residential units plus 43 EWS units). Both registrations are dated 02.05.2025 (brochure page 63).

cards.label: Chinju No Mori
cards.body: The Forest Reserve is built on the Japanese concept of Chinju No Mori — a sacred grove. Brochure page 20.

cards.label: Thirteen design principles
cards.body: Minamoto, Engawa, Kaiyu Sei, Shakkei, Mie Gakure, In-ei, Ten Sen Men, Seijaku, Miyama, Kagami, Kekkai, Koshi, Oku. Brochure pages 22–30.

cards.label: Sector 36A, Gurugram
cards.body: Krisumi City Group Housing Colony, on 6.4868 acres across Phase I and Phase II. Brochure page 63.

cards.label: Five Krisumi Way principles
cards.body: Wa (Harmony), Omotenashi (Hospitality), Monozukuri (Craftsmanship), Seijitsu (Integrity), Iki (Sophistication). Brochure page 3.

faqs.question: Where is The Forest Reserve located?
faqs.answer: The Forest Reserve is located in Sector 36A, Gurugram, as part of the Krisumi City Group Housing Colony. The brochure's location map (page 60) lists Indira Gandhi International Airport at 24.8 km, Cyber City at 17 km, Medanta Hospital at 9.6 km, and Northern Peripheral Road (Dwarka Expressway) at 1 km from the site.

faqs.question: Who is the developer?
faqs.answer: The Forest Reserve and The Forest Reserve - II are developed by Krisumi Corporation Private Limited. The brochure describes Krisumi as a partnership between Krishna Group India and Sumitomo Corporation Japan (brochure pages 4, 5, 6, 7). Architecture is by Nikken Sekkei, Japan's No. 1 architectural firm (brochure page 7).

faqs.question: What configurations are available?
faqs.answer: The brochure references 2 LDK, 3 LDK, 4 LDK and Penthouse residences across three building designations — The Forest Reserve A, The Forest Reserve B (one combined RERA registration covering A and B), and The Forest Reserve II (separately registered).

faqs.question: What is the RERA registration number?
faqs.answer: The Forest Reserve is registered with the Haryana Real Estate Regulatory Authority (HARERA Gurugram) under two registration numbers, both dated 02.05.2025: The Forest Reserve — RC/REP/HARERA/GGM/944/676/2025/47, and The Forest Reserve - II — RC/REP/HARERA/GGM/945/677/2025/48 (brochure page 63). Details are verifiable at haryanarera.gov.in under 'Registered Projects'.

faqs.question: What is the project area and unit count?
faqs.answer: Per the brochure RERA disclosure (page 63), The Forest Reserve covers 5.1743 acres with 298 residential units and 53 EWS units. The Forest Reserve - II covers 1.3125 acres with 244 residential units and 43 EWS units. Combined: 6.4868 acres, 542 residential units, and 96 EWS units.

faqs.question: What is the design concept?
faqs.answer: The Forest Reserve is built on the Japanese concept of Chinju No Mori — a grove or forest that serves as a sacred haven for kami, the divine spirits that safeguard the land and its people (brochure page 20). The brochure expresses this through thirteen Japanese design principles across pages 22 to 30: Minamoto, Engawa, Kaiyu Sei, Shakkei, Mie Gakure, In-ei, Ten Sen Men, Seijaku, Miyama, Kagami, Kekkai, Koshi, and Oku.

faqs.question: What amenities are exclusive to The Forest Reserve?
faqs.answer: Brochure page 58 lists three amenities exclusive to The Forest Reserve, on the ground floor: Lap Pool, PDR Dining (Private Dining Room), and Gym. All Krisumi City residents share the common-area amenities listed on brochure page 57: Pool Bar, Outdoor Gazebo, Waterscape, Multipurpose Hall, Amphitheatre, Kid's Stream, Kid's Water Park, Kid's Play Area, Mist Pond, Resting Place, and Source Fountain.

faqs.question: What are the Krisumi Way principles?
faqs.answer: Brochure page 3 names five Krisumi Way principles: Wa (Harmony), Omotenashi (Hospitality), Monozukuri (Craftsmanship), Seijitsu (Integrity), and Iki (Sophistication).

faqs.question: How does The Forest Reserve fit within Krisumi City?
faqs.answer: Per brochure page 63, The Forest Reserve and The Forest Reserve - II are part of the Group Housing Colony named 'Krisumi City' in Sector 36A, Gurugram. Brochure pages 6 and 7 introduce the broader Krisumi portfolio: Waterfall Residences, Waterfall Suites, Waterfall Suites II, and Waterside Residences.

faqs.question: Is the price available?
faqs.answer: Detailed pricing is not published in the brochure. For current pricing, payment plans, and unit availability, please contact Krisumi via the brochure's listed channels — sales@krisumi.com, +91 9513270083, +91 87226 87226 — or via Cornerhouse Realty.

disclaimerAddendum: The Forest Reserve and The Forest Reserve - II are being developed by Krisumi Corporation Private Limited as part of the Krisumi City Group Housing Colony in Sector 36A, Gurugram, Haryana. HARERA Gurugram Registration Numbers, both dated 2 May 2025: RC/REP/HARERA/GGM/944/676/2025/47 (The Forest Reserve, on 5.1743 acres comprising 298 residential units and 53 EWS units) and RC/REP/HARERA/GGM/945/677/2025/48 (The Forest Reserve - II, on 1.3125 acres comprising 244 residential units and 43 EWS units). Information published on this page is sourced from the Krisumi brochure for The Forest Reserve. Pricing and unit-specific details are available on request from the developer. Renders, layouts and amenity references are conceptual artist's impressions for representational purposes; actual designs are subject to authority approvals. Prospective purchasers are advised to verify all approved details on the HARERA website at haryanarera.gov.in before booking.
